hooks.//: C-3/M-5 last-resort PII tripwire. Reads .tool_input.file_path AND a grep -c count of PII-class matches over .tool_input.content. awk applies the same strict basename allowlist as ultra-analyzer (rejects any shell-meta in the path) and only proceeds when the count is non-zero. Emits ONLY a printf %q WARNING (path quoted against shell-meta). Invokes no external tools, mutates nothing, and ALWAYS exits 0 - it never blocks the Write. Primary PII defense is in-skill redaction (references/pii-policy.md); this is the safety net.
hooks.command: input=$(cat); fp=$(printf '%s' "$input" | jq -r '(.tool_input.file_path // "")'); n=$(printf '%s' "$input" | jq -r '(.tool_input.content // "")' | grep -aciE '(bearer[ ]+[A-Za-z0-9._-]+|eyJ[A-Za-z0-9._-]{10,}|authorization["'\'' :=]+|(set-)?cookie["'\'' :=]+|[A-Za-z0-9._%+-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.-]+[.][A-Za-z]{2,}|[+]?[0-9][0-9 ()-]{8,}[0-9])'); printf '%s\n' "$fp" | awk -v n="$n" '$0 !~ /[^A-Za-z0-9_.\/-]/ && $0 !~ /[`$;&|<>(){}*?~!]/ && length($0)>0 && length($0)<=4096 && n+0>0 {print}' | while IFS= read -r f; do [ -n "$f" ] && printf '[hook] WARNING: possible PII in %q (last-resort tripwire only; redact at source - references/pii-policy.md)\n' "$f"; done; exit 0
